# http-errors

[![NPM Version][npm-version-image]][npm-url]
[![NPM Downloads][npm-downloads-image]][npm-url]
[![Node.js Version][node-version-image]][node-version-url]
[![Build Status][travis-image]][travis-url]
[![Test Coverage][coveralls-image]][coveralls-url]

Create HTTP errors for Express.js and other Node.js applications

## Installation

```sh
$ npm install http-errors
```

## Example

```js
var createError = require('http-errors')
var express = require('express')
var app = express()

app.use(function (req, res, next) {
  if (!req.user) return next(createError(401, 'Please login to view this page.'))
  next()
})

app.get('/evening', function (req, res) {
  res.status(200).type('text/plain').send('Good evening\n')
})

app.use(function (err, req, res, next) {
  res.status(err.status || 500)
  res.send(err.message)
})
```

## API

### createError([status], [message], [properties])

Create a new error object with the given message and status code. The error object inherits from `Error`.

```js
var err = createError(404, 'This page is not found')
```

- `status`: The HTTP status code. Defaults to 500.
- `message`: The error message. Defaults to the standard message for the status code.
- `properties`: Additional properties to attach to the error object.

### createError.HttpError

The base error class for all HTTP errors created by this module. Inherits from `Error`.

### createError.isHttpError(val)

Returns `true` if `val` is an instance of `HttpError`, `false` otherwise.

### createError[status]

A shorthand function for creating an error with a specific status code. The message is set to the standard message for the status code.

```js
var notFound = createError.NotFound()
var badRequest = createError.BadRequest('Invalid syntax')
```

## List of all status constructors

### 4xx Client Error

- `createError.BadRequest` - 400
- `createError.Unauthorized` - 401
- `createError.PaymentRequired` - 402
- `createError.Forbidden` - 403
- `createError.NotFound` - 404
- `createError.MethodNotAllowed` - 405
- `createError.NotAcceptable` - 406
- `createError.ProxyAuthenticationRequired` - 407
- `createError.RequestTimeout` - 408
- `createError.Conflict` - 409
- `createError.Gone` - 410
- `createError.LengthRequired` - 411
- `createError.PreconditionFailed` - 412
- `createError.PayloadTooLarge` - 413
- `createError.URITooLong` - 414
- `createError.UnsupportedMediaType` - 415
- `createError.RangeNotSatisfiable` - 416
- `createError.ExpectationFailed` - 417
- `createError.ImATeapot` - 418
- `createError.MisdirectedRequest` - 421
- `createError.UnprocessableEntity` - 422
- `createError.Locked` - 423
- `createError.FailedDependency` - 424
- `createError.UnorderedCollection` - 425
- `createError.UpgradeRequired` - 426
- `createError.PreconditionRequired` - 428
- `createError.TooManyRequests` - 429
- `createError.RequestHeaderFieldsTooLarge` - 431
- `createError.UnavailableForLegalReasons` - 451

### 5xx Server Error

- `createError.InternalServerError` - 500
- `createError.NotImplemented` - 501
- `createError.BadGateway` - 502
- `createError.ServiceUnavailable` - 503
- `createError.GatewayTimeout` - 504
- `createError.HTTPVersionNotSupported` - 505
- `createError.VariantAlsoNegotiates` - 506
- `createError.InsufficientStorage` - 507
- `createError.LoopDetected` - 508
- `createError.BandwidthLimitExceeded` - 509
- `createError.NotExtended` - 510
- `createError.NetworkAuthenticationRequired` - 511

## Express.js Error Handling Example

```js
var createError = require('http-errors')
var express = require('express')
var app = express()

// Routes
app.get('/', function (req, res) {
  res.status(200).type('text/plain').send('Hello, World!\n')
})

app.get('/evening', function (req, res) {
  res.status(200).type('text/plain').send('Good evening\n')
})

// Route that triggers a 404 error
app.get('/not-found', function (req, res, next) {
  next(createError(404, 'This page does not exist'))
})

// Route that triggers a 500 error
app.get('/server-error', function (req, res, next) {
  next(createError(500, 'Internal server error occurred'))
})

// Error handling middleware
app.use(function (err, req, res, next) {
  // Set locals, only providing error in development
  res.locals.message = err.message
  res.locals.error = req.app.get('env') === 'development' ? err : {}

  // Set status and respond with error message
  res.status(err.status || 500)
  res.type('text/plain').send(err.message)
})

app.listen(3000, '127.0.0.1', function () {
  console.log('Server running at http://127.0.0.1:3000/')
})
```

## Using with Custom Error Objects

You can extend the `HttpError` class to create your own custom error types:

```js
var createError = require('http-errors')
var util = require('util')

// Create a custom error type
function CustomError(message) {
  // Call the parent constructor
  createError.InternalServerError.call(this, message)
  
  // Set the name for stack traces
  this.name = 'CustomError'
  Error.captureStackTrace(this, CustomError)
}

// Inherit from InternalServerError
util.inherits(CustomError, createError.InternalServerError)

// Use in your application
app.get('/custom-error', function (req, res, next) {
  next(new CustomError('A custom error occurred'))
})
```
